subject: 英語（中学）
question:

---
Dear Diary,
Today was really busy. I woke up at 6:30 and had breakfast with my family. After that, I helped my mom clean the kitchen. In the afternoon, I went to the library to study for my math test. I came home at 5:00 p.m. and cooked dinner with my sister. We had a great time together.
---

Hi Emma,
How are you? I'm writing to tell you about our new house. We moved last weekend. Our new address is 15 Oak Street, near Green Park. The nearest train station is Park Station. It takes only five minutes on foot.
Come and visit us soon! My mom will make your favorite chocolate cake.
Best wishes,
Lily
---

Sarah Lee

Your friend,
Tom

Dear Amy,
I hope you are well! I am having a birthday party at my house this Saturday. It starts at 3:00 p.m. I have invited five of our friends. We will play games, eat cake, and watch a movie.
I would really love it if you could come! Please let me know by Thursday.
Your friend,
Mia

Hi everyone,
As you know, our school has a new recycling program. Starting next month, we will have separate bins for paper, plastic, and cans in every classroom. I want to ask all students to help us keep our school clean and reduce waste. Small actions can make a big difference. Please think about the environment every day.
Ms. Carter, Science Teacher
---

Love,
Kenji

Sophie

I want to write back to her right away!

Emily

School Library Borrowing Rules
- Students may borrow up to 3 books at a time.
- Books must be returned within 2 weeks.
- You may renew a book once for an extra 2 weeks.
- Students with overdue books cannot borrow new books.
- Reference books and magazines must stay in the library.

Dear James,
Thank you for your message. Our school tennis team has two practice sessions per week — on Tuesdays and Thursdays, from 4:00 to 6:00 p.m. New members are welcome to join any time. However, please note that you will need your own racket, as we do not have extras. The coach is Ms. Reed. Feel free to come and watch a practice session first before you decide to join.
Best,
Team Captain Alex
---

Students are encouraged to reduce their use of plastic bottles by bringing their own water bottles. We believe small steps can help protect our environment for future generations.
